DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jesse Contreras, Esq. 310-564-2670 of Michelman & Robinson LLP for Plaintiff.
Chet Kronenberg, Esq. 310-407-7557 Bryan Jin, Esq. 650-251-5068 of Simpson Thacher for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VISTA EQUITY PARTNERS MANAGEMENT, LLC, VISTA EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

Defendants Vista Equity Partners Management, LLC and Vista Equity Partners, LLC's ("Vista") demurrer to complaint is off calendar as moot.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jesse Contreras, Esq. 310-564-2670 of Michelman & Robinson LLP for Plaintiff.
Chet Kronenberg, Esq. 310-407-7557 Bryan Jin, Esq. 650-251-5068 of Simpson Thacher for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT VISTA EQUITY PARTNERS MANAGEMENT, LLC, VISTA EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC'S MOTION TO DISMISS BASED ON THE DOCTRINE OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO STAY THIS ACTION PENDING RESOLUTION OF A RELATED DELAWARE ACTION.

The Cout adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Vista Equity Partners Management, LLC and Vista Equity Partners, LLC's ("Vista") motion to dismiss based on forum con conveniens is granted.

Plaintiff's stock option agreement contains an enforceable mandatory forum selection clause that mandates that this dispute be litigated in Delaware. (Complaint, Ex. 3, 20.) Non-signatories Vista are intended third-party beneficiaries of the agreement. The allegations of the complaint demonstrate that Vista are "Investor Funds" that can enforce the agreement. (Complaint, Ex. 3, 2(c)(ii) and 5; Complaint, 5, 17, 22, 24, 25, 41, 44.)

In addition, Vista can enforce the agreement because it is "closely related" to the contractual relationship. (See Bugna v. Fike (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 229, 235 ["The key to the closely related test is whether the nonsignatories were close to the contractual relationship"]; Lu v. Dryclean-U.S.A. of California, Inc. (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 1490, 1494 [" '[A] range of transaction participants, parties and non-parties, should benefit from and be subject to forum selection clauses.'"]; see also Berclain America Latina v. Baan Co. (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 401, 408.) Indeed, plaintiff expressly alleges in the complaint that Vista were the entities that orchestrated Solera's breach of the Board Member Services Agreement and Stock Option Agreement. (See also Opp. at 1:6-8 ["Vista controls the majority of directors sitting on the Solera Global Board of Directors. . . . Vista caused Solera Holdings to terminate Solera board member Kurt Lauk . . . ."].)

Plaintiff-who has already filed suit against Solera in federal court in Delaware-fails to show that enforcement of the forum selection clause would be unreasonable. Plaintiff therefore has no basis to avoid it. (See Korman v. Princess Cruise Lines, Ltd (2019) 32 Cal.App.5th 206, 213 [forum selection clause is presumed valid and will be enforced unless the plaintiff shows that enforcement of the clause would be unreasonable under the circumstances of the case]; Drulias v. 1st Century Bancshares, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 696, 703 [the party seeking to avoid enforcement of a contractual forum selection clause bears the substantial burden of proving why it should not be enforced].) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
